title: Hut 8 (HUT) Q1 Loss Of US$219.8 Million Tests Bullish Growth Narratives
text: Hut 8's Q1 2026 results reveal a significant tension between growth expectations and profitability challenges. The company reported a net loss excluding extra items of US$219.8 million in Q1 2026, representing a substantial widening from the prior year's comparable loss of $133.9 million despite revenue more than tripling from $21.8 million to $71.0 million. On a trailing twelve-month basis, the company accumulated losses exceeding $311 million against $284.3 million in revenue. This creates a fundamental mismatch: higher contracted coverage has not translated into positive earnings, undermining the bullish thesis that long-term power and infrastructure deals would stabilize margins. Valuation Disconnect: The stock trades at a price-to-sales multiple of 43.1x versus the software industry average of 3.7x. Analysts forecast 31.85% annual revenue growth yet expect the company to remain unprofitable through at least 2029, creating a scenario where investors pay premium valuations for growth without near-term profitability visibility. While revenue expansion is genuine, the persistent and widening losses challenge narratives that contractual stability would quickly restore profitability.
